title: Hex Dek - a hex map in a deck of cards (and a stealth Design Diary for the Land in Peril RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wu4lafiHjh0

Daniel (@dungeondive) looks at three Philip Reed card decks — Hex Dek, 50 Patrons of the Market, and 50 Guests of the Inn — system-agnostic tools for generating adventures on the fly. Hex Dek is the star: 50 double-sided cards, each a seven-hex tile with terrain and one or two keyed locations plus a d6 encounter table. Draw one for an instant seven-hex solo adventure, or deal hands to players so they collaboratively build the campaign map — a nice way to hand worldbuilding agency to the table. The two NPC decks supply ready-made market and inn characters, each with a story hook to break the dreaded blank-mind moment.

The back half is a stealth design diary for his own A Land in Peril RPG, built in tandem with the board game and designed to play nicely with exactly these agnostic tools. He details its dice-pool challenges, the determination stat doubling as HP and authorship currency, and a borrowed-from-Talisman "chaos die" feeding a Boon/Bane chaos bag oracle that forces the solo player to drive the story for good or ill, plus on-the-fly tables for rolling up enemy type, weakness and temperament. A tools-and-design double feature.

----

Hex Dek can be dealt out so players collectively build the map, handing worldbuilding to the whole table. Do you like sharing that authorship around, or do you prefer one person (or the dice) to own the world?
